proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 122 anonymized features, at least one binary, ordinal, or continuous variable will show a statistically significant association with objective_response in a univariate screen.
feature_051 (integer-valued 0/1/2, plausibly an ECOG performance status) is inversely associated with objective_response across the full cohort.
feature_092 (continuous in [0, 0.8], plausibly PD-L1 tumor proportion score) is positively associated with objective_response across the full cohort.

analyses.code: for c in features: chi2 (binary/categorical) or pointbiserialr (continuous) vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Univariate screen of 122 features against objective_response (n=50000, ORR=16.9%). Strongest signals: feature_051 r=-0.092 (p=7.9e-95), feature_013 binary diff=-4.2pp (p=1.1e-33), feature_011 r=-0.053 (p=1.2e-32), feature_067 binary diff=-3.4pp (p=2.0e-18), feature_006 binary diff=+1.8pp (p=1.0e-6), feature_099 r=+0.017 (p=1.4e-4), feature_007 binary diff=+1.2pp (p=5.9e-4), feature_063 r=-0.015 (p=6.8e-4), feature_092 r=+0.013 (p=3.2e-3), feature_039 binary diff=+1.0pp (p=3.4e-3). Many features have no marginal association.
feature_051 is strongly inversely associated with objective_response (r=-0.0922, p=7.9e-95). ORR drops monotonically from 0.197 at value 0 to 0.151 at value 1 to 0.108 at value 2.
feature_092 marginal correlation with objective_response is small (r=+0.013, p=3.2e-3). The marginal signal is weak, suggesting the effect may be conditional on other variables (e.g., treatment).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the top univariate predictors in a multivariable logistic regression, feature_051, feature_013, feature_011, feature_067, feature_006, feature_099, feature_007, feature_063, feature_092, and feature_039 retain independent associations with objective_response.
Categorical variables for race (feature_123) and insurance (feature_005) do not have substantive independent effects on objective_response after adjustment for clinical/treatment features.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic with 14 features + categoricals. All top binaries/continuous remain significant: feature_051 beta=-0.381 (p<1e-94), feature_013 beta=-0.302 (p<1e-33), feature_011 beta=-0.039 (p<1e-32), feature_067 beta=-0.259 (p<1e-18), feature_006 beta=+0.131 (p<1e-5), feature_099 beta=+0.093 (p<1e-3), feature_007 beta=+0.080 (p=0.001), feature_063 beta=-0.005 (p=5e-4), feature_092 beta=+0.174 (p=0.002), feature_039 beta=+0.077 (p=0.001).

analyses.code: Joint Wald tests for race and insurance dummies in adjusted Logit
analyses.result_summary: Race and insurance dummies are not jointly significant in adjusted model: race effects all p>0.1; insurance only private vs medicaid p=0.02 (likely chance). No major disparities in residual ORR after clinical adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (binary, 28.5% prevalence, positive marginal association with ORR) functions as a treatment indicator whose effect on objective_response is enhanced by feature_092 (PD-L1).
The interaction between feature_006 and feature_092 on objective_response is positive and statistically significant.

analyses.result_summary: feature_006 x feature_092 interaction: beta=+0.594, SE=0.122, z=4.86, p=1.2e-6. Main effects for both are non-significant; the interaction term carries the signal. Within feature_006=1 the PD-L1 correlation with ORR is r=+0.047 (p=1.4e-8); within feature_006=0 it is r=-0.001 (p=0.79).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 jointly identify a regimen: only when all three equal 1 is objective_response elevated above baseline (consistent with a 3-component treatment indicator pattern).
The 3-way interaction term feature_006 x feature_007 x feature_039 is positive and significant in a logistic regression including all main effects and pairwise interactions.

analyses.result_summary: Cross-tab of 8 cells: ORR = 0.161, 0.162, 0.165, 0.168, 0.169, 0.169, 0.165, 0.230 for cells (000)-(111). Only cell (1,1,1), n=3216, has elevated ORR (22.97% vs ~16.5% in other 7 cells).

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, [const, a, b, c, ab, ac, bc, abc]).fit()  # a=f006, b=f007, c=f039
analyses.result_summary: All main effects and 2-way interactions are non-significant. The 3-way term abc has beta=+0.391, SE=0.105, z=3.74, p=1.8e-4. The signal is concentrated in the (1,1,1) cell - consistent with a triple-component treatment regimen.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the (feature_006=1, feature_007=1, feature_039=1) triple-positive subgroup ('triplet_111', n=3216), feature_092 (PD-L1) is strongly positively associated with objective_response, while in all other subgroups it is not.
A binary high-PD-L1 indicator (feature_092 >= 0.50) yields ORR > 40% in triplet_111 patients vs ~17% in all other treatment cells - i.e., the IO benefit is essentially restricted to PD-L1 high.

analyses.code: for each triplet cell: pointbiserialr(feature_092, objective_response)
analyses.result_summary: Within triplet_111: r=+0.237, p=2.2e-42 (n=3216). Within all other 7 cells: r ranges -0.028 to +0.012, all p>0.07. PD-L1 association with response is exclusive to triplet_111.

analyses.code: Within triplet_111: ORR by feature_092>=0.5; logit(y, [t111, high_pdl1, t111*high_pdl1])
analyses.result_summary: triplet_111 + feature_092>=0.5: ORR=44.8% (n=712). triplet_111 + feature_092<0.5: ORR=16.8% (n=2504). Logit interaction t111*high_pdl1: beta=+1.405, SE=0.097, z=14.4, p=3e-47. PD-L1 high yields a +28pp absolute ORR boost, but only in the triplet_111 group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (ordinal 0/1/2, candidate ECOG performance status) is independently and dose-dependently inversely associated with objective_response after adjustment for treatment and other prognostic variables.

analyses.code: Adjusted logit with feature_051 + triplet + PD-L1 + other covariates
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 adjusted beta=-0.383, SE=0.018, z=-20.8, p<1e-95. ORR by score: 0->0.197, 1->0.151, 2->0.108 (overall, before adjustment). Effect persists across treatment subgroups: in triplet_111 ORR by score is 0.270, 0.213, 0.187. Strongly prognostic, modestly modifies treatment effect (interaction p=0.06).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (binary, 64.7% prevalence, plausibly an indicator of metastatic/extensive disease) is independently associated with lower objective_response.

The negative effect of feature_013 on ORR is similar in magnitude across all 8 (feature_006, feature_007, feature_039) treatment cells, consistent with a patient prognostic factor rather than an effect modifier.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013 adjusted beta=-0.305, SE=0.025, z=-12.4, p<1e-34. Marginal ORR: 0.196 (f013=0) vs 0.154 (f013=1).

Across all 8 triplet cells, ORR drops by ~3.5-7.4 pp going from feature_013=0 to feature_013=1. The interaction term t111*feature_013 in a logit is beta=+0.167 (p=0.064). Borderline; effect is broadly consistent across treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (binary, 25.2% prevalence) is independently and negatively associated with objective_response (candidate adverse clinical feature, e.g., presence of brain or liver metastases).

analyses.result_summary: feature_067 adjusted beta=-0.260, SE=0.029, z=-8.93, p<1e-18. Marginal ORR: 0.177 (f067=0) vs 0.144 (f067=1). Independent of feature_013 and feature_051.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (continuous 0-28, plausibly pack-years smoking) is negatively associated with objective_response and the effect is approximately linear on the logit scale.

analyses.result_summary: feature_011 adjusted beta=-0.0397, SE=0.003, z=-12.12, p<1e-33. Per-unit OR=0.961; per-SD OR ~0.83. No interaction with triplet_111 (interaction p=0.72), so it is a prognostic covariate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous 1.7-5.5, plausibly serum albumin) is positively associated with objective_response after adjustment.
feature_063 (continuous, heavily right-skewed, plausibly an inflammatory marker like CRP) is negatively associated with objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: feature_099 adjusted beta=+0.092, SE=0.024, z=3.83, p=1.3e-4. OR per unit ~1.10, consistent with albumin as a positive prognostic factor.
feature_063 adjusted beta=-0.0052, SE=0.0015, z=-3.55, p=4e-4. Effect is small per unit but consistent direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Histology (feature_043: adenocarcinoma vs squamous) does not modify the effect of triplet_111 treatment on objective_response.
Smoking status (feature_057: never/former/current) does not modify the effect of triplet_111 on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Histology x triplet_111 interaction: beta=+0.044, SE=0.092, p=0.63. Treatment effect of triplet_111 is similar in adenocarcinoma and squamous histologies. Marginal ORR by histology is also similar (adeno 16.7% vs squamous 17.4%, p=0.08).

Smoking x triplet_111 interaction: never vs former p=0.42, current vs former p=0.57. No effect modification by smoking status. ORR by smoking is also similar (never 16.4%, former 16.8%, current 17.2%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_123) does not differentially affect either the probability of receiving triplet_111 or the ORR achieved on triplet_111 - i.e., no major demographic disparities in IO access or response.
Insurance type (feature_005) does not differentially affect triplet_111 receipt or response.

analyses.result_summary: Triplet_111 receipt by race is uniform: white 6.5%, black 6.2%, hispanic 6.4%, asian 6.6%, other 5.8%. Within triplet_111, ORR by race is uniform: white 22.6%, black 25.5%, hispanic 21.9%, asian 23.5%, other 27.1%; chi-square p>0.4. No detectable disparity.

Triplet_111 receipt by insurance: medicaid 6.6%, medicare 6.7%, private 6.1%, uninsured 6.5%. Within triplet_111, ORR: medicaid 22.6%, medicare 22.0%, private 24.0%, uninsured 25.6%. No differences detectable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Performance status (feature_051) modifies the absolute response benefit of triplet_111 such that the absolute treatment effect is largest in feature_051=0 patients (best PS) and smallest in feature_051=2.
feature_013 (metastatic-like indicator) is broadly prognostic but does not strongly modify the triplet_111 treatment effect on the multiplicative scale.

analyses.result_summary: t111 x feature_051 interaction beta=+0.126, SE=0.066, p=0.056. Borderline; absolute ORR difference (t111 vs other) is largest at PS=0 (+7.3pp) and decreases at PS=2 (+8pp). On odds scale interaction is small.
t111 x feature_013 interaction beta=+0.167, SE=0.090, p=0.064. Borderline; effect of triplet_111 is similar in feature_013=0 and feature_013=1 patients on the OR scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among binary features not already identified as treatment indicators, there exists at least one feature whose interaction with triplet_111 strongly modifies treatment effect (negative or positive).
Specifically, feature_027 (binary, 14.9% prevalence) attenuates or abolishes the response benefit of triplet_111, consistent with a driver-mutation/resistance biomarker.

analyses.code: For each binary feature: Logit(y, [const, t111, f, t111*f]); rank by p_int
analyses.result_summary: Strongest interaction: feature_027 x triplet_111, beta=-0.865, SE=0.151, p=1.1e-8. Other interactions all p>0.008. feature_027 stands out as the dominant treatment-effect modifier.

Cross-tab: t111=0,f027=0 ORR=16.4% (n=39798); t111=0,f027=1 ORR=16.8% (n=6986); t111=1,f027=0 ORR=24.8% (n=2751); t111=1,f027=1 ORR=12.5% (n=465). In feature_027=1 patients, triplet_111 not only fails to improve response, it appears modestly harmful (p=0.004 for treatment effect comparison in this stratum).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PD-L1 (feature_092) effect within triplet_111 is itself modified by feature_027: in feature_027=0 patients, high PD-L1 confers a large response boost, whereas in feature_027=1 patients PD-L1 expression carries no prognostic information.
A 3-way interaction triplet_111 x high_PD-L1 x feature_027 is significantly negative, capturing the loss of biomarker-driven IO benefit in feature_027-positive disease.

analyses.result_summary: Triplet_111 + high PD-L1 + feature_027=0: ORR=48.9% (n=617). Triplet_111 + high PD-L1 + feature_027=1: ORR=17.9% (n=95). Triplet_111 + low PD-L1 + feature_027=0: ORR=17.8% (n=2134). Triplet_111 + low PD-L1 + feature_027=1: ORR=11.1% (n=370). High PD-L1 boosts response only in triplet_111+feature_027=0.

analyses.code: Logit with t111*high_pdl1*feature_027 plus all lower-order terms
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction beta=-0.910, SE=0.341, p=0.008. Two-way t111*high_pdl1 beta=+1.499, p<1e-46. Two-way t111*feature_027 beta=-0.582, p=0.001. Confirms that the IO/PD-L1 benefit is abolished by feature_027.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_027=1 patients, no other binary 'treatment-like' feature produces a clinically meaningful (>5pp) ORR boost - i.e., this dataset does not contain a corresponding targeted-therapy indicator that would be expected to help feature_027-positive patients.

analyses.code: For feature_027==1 subset: ORR by each binary feature (n>=50 each)
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_027=1 (n=7451, ORR=16.5%), the largest positive ORR difference for any single binary is +2.5pp (feature_111). No feature produces a >5pp boost. Top hurters are general prognostic factors (feature_013 -3.2pp, feature_067 -3.0pp, feature_049 -3.1pp). No targeted-therapy-like signal is present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous lab markers other than feature_092 (PD-L1), feature_099 (albumin), and feature_063 (CRP) have negligible independent associations with objective_response.

analyses.code: Univariate logit per SD for each continuous lab
analyses.result_summary: Among 30 lab-like continuous features, only feature_099 (p=1.4e-4, OR/SD=1.05) and feature_063 (p=6.8e-4, OR/SD=0.96) reach p<0.001 in marginal analysis. All others (feature_055, 070, 044, 084, 028, 065, 056, 103, 094, 121, 019, 059, 003, 010, 054, 062, 031, 082, 097, 077, 014, 023, 096, 119, 090, 020, 101, 118) have p>0.08. Lab panel is mostly uninformative for ORR prediction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After triplet_111 treatment effect is captured, no additional binary 'treatment-like' feature (e.g., feature_086, feature_115, feature_002, feature_037, feature_053, feature_093) carries an independent positive ORR signal.

analyses.result_summary: feature_086, feature_115, feature_002, feature_037, feature_053, feature_093 all have univariate p>0.05 against ORR and lose any signal in adjusted models. feature_006, 007, 039 are the only binary features whose 3-way combination identifies a high-response cell. No 4-way or other multi-component treatment combination is detectable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (PD-L1) has an apparent threshold effect: ORR is roughly flat below ~0.5 and rises sharply above 0.5 within triplet_111 patients.

analyses.code: Within triplet_111: pd.qcut(feature_092, 5); ORR by quintile
analyses.result_summary: Within triplet_111 (n=3216), ORR by PD-L1 quintile: Q1 17.2%, Q2 17.4%, Q3 15.2%, Q4 18.0%, Q5 47.0%. Marked threshold near the top quintile (cutpoint ~0.5). Suggests a discrete 'high PD-L1' biomarker stratum drives the IO benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable logistic model including triplet_111, its interactions with feature_092 and feature_027, and the major prognostic covariates (feature_051, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_099, feature_063) captures the dominant ORR signal in this cohort.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, [const, t111, t111*pdl1, t111*f027, t111*pdl1*f027, pdl1, f027, pdl1*f027, ecog, f013, f067, py, alb, crp]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final model: t111 beta=-0.515 (p<1e-6), t111*pdl1 beta=+3.05 (p<1e-37), t111*pdl1*feature_027 beta=-1.89 (p=0.011), feature_051 beta=-0.383 (p<1e-95), feature_013 beta=-0.305 (p<1e-34), feature_067 beta=-0.260 (p<1e-18), feature_011 beta=-0.040 (p<1e-32), feature_099 beta=+0.092 (p=1e-4), feature_063 beta=-0.005 (p=4e-4). Pseudo R^2=0.026.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among triplet_111-treated patients, the ORR benefit is concentrated in the feature_092>=0.5 AND feature_027=0 stratum, while the feature_027=1 AND feature_092<0.5 stratum experiences a small absolute ORR decrement compared to non-triplet_111 controls.

analyses.code: Stratified ORR comparison: t111 vs control by (feature_092>=0.5, feature_027) cells
analyses.result_summary: feature_027=0 + high PD-L1: t111 ORR=48.9% vs control 16.3%, diff=+32.6pp, p~1e-89. feature_027=0 + low PD-L1: t111 ORR=17.8% vs control 16.4%, diff=+1.3pp, p=0.12. feature_027=1 + low PD-L1: t111 ORR=11.1% vs control 16.9%, diff=-5.8pp, p=0.004. feature_027=1 + high PD-L1: t111 ORR=17.9% vs control 16.5%, diff=+1.4pp, p=0.83. Treatment effect is dichotomous: large benefit only in PD-L1-high mutation-negative; minimal or harmful elsewhere.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pairwise 2-way interaction terms 006*007, 006*039, 007*039 are each individually significant in 2-feature logits but become non-significant once the 3-way interaction is included, supporting the interpretation that all three components must be present together.

analyses.code: Compare logits with each 2-way interaction alone vs full 3-way model
analyses.result_summary: feature_006*feature_007: beta=+0.149 p=0.004 (alone); becomes -0.060 p=0.43 (with 3-way included). feature_006*feature_039: beta=+0.184 p=4e-4 alone; -0.004 p=0.96 with 3-way. feature_007*feature_039: beta=+0.146 p=0.002 alone; +0.023 p=0.68 with 3-way. The 3-way term beta=+0.391 p=2e-4 absorbs all pairwise apparent signals.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Restricting to the feature_027=0 majority (n=42549), the triplet_111 x feature_092 interaction remains highly significant and quantitatively similar, demonstrating the robustness of the biomarker-driven IO benefit estimate.

analyses.code: Within feature_027==0: Logit(y, [const, t111, pdl1, t111*pdl1])
analyses.result_summary: In feature_027=0 subset: ORR triplet_111+high PD-L1 = 48.9% (n=617); other cells in this stratum 16-18%. Logit interaction t111*pdl1 in this subgroup beta~+1.62 SE 0.10 p<1e-50. The biomarker-driven benefit replicates with similar magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Major prognostic covariates (feature_051, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_099, feature_063) are not differentially distributed across treatment cells, supporting that the triplet_111 effect is not confounded by indication.

analyses.result_summary: feature_051 mean: t111=0 -> 0.99, t111=1 -> 1.00. feature_013 prev: 64.6% vs 64.4%. feature_067 prev: 25.2% vs 25.1%. feature_011 mean: 3.84 vs 3.85. feature_099 mean: 3.80 vs 3.80. feature_092 mean: 0.31 vs 0.31. feature_027 prev: 14.9% vs 14.5%. Treatment cells are well-balanced on measured covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Synthesis: The dominant signal in this NSCLC cohort is a treatment-biomarker interaction in which a 3-component regimen (jointly indicated by feature_006=feature_007=feature_039=1) yields a large objective response benefit only in patients with high feature_092 (PD-L1 >=0.5) and absence of feature_027 (driver-mutation/resistance marker). Performance status (feature_051), metastatic-burden indicator (feature_013), feature_067, smoking exposure (feature_011), albumin (feature_099), and CRP-like (feature_063) are independent prognostic factors; histology, smoking status, race, and insurance do not modify treatment effect.

analyses.code: Final consolidation; predicted ORR for canonical strata
analyses.result_summary: Canonical strata (model-implied / observed): triplet_111 + high PD-L1 + feature_027=0 -> ORR ~49%. triplet_111 + low PD-L1 + feature_027=0 -> ORR ~18%. triplet_111 + feature_027=1 -> ORR 11-18% (no benefit). Non-triplet_111 -> ORR ~16-17%. Prognostic gradient by feature_051: 0 -> 19.7%, 1 -> 15.1%, 2 -> 10.8%. The biomarker-defined benefit is the central, robust finding.
